Apartment Daytime Cooled Population Sweep

Deterministic apartment population sweep using the traced apartment template, three occupant cohorts, and a daytime cooled room-environment matrix with orientation-based MRT offsets.

Case Setup

Bottom of plan is south. Daytime cooled apartment condition.

North/interior rooms use Tr=Ta, south-facing rooms use Tr=Ta+1.5 C, east/west-exposed rooms use Tr=Ta+0.75 C.

TagOccupied RoomExposure Rule
LRLiving Roomnorth_interior
DRDining Roomnorth_interior
PBPrimary Bedroomeast_west
KKitchennorth_interior
BNBreakfast Nooknorth_interior
STStudynorth_interior
FWFamily Lounge Westnorth_interior
FEFamily Lounge Eastsouth
GRGuest Roomsouth
B1Bedroom 1south
B2Bedroom 2south
B3Bedroom 3south

Auto Findings

  • Young Scholars Mixed: highest mean PMV at young_mixed_t25p0_rh75, highest single-room PPD at young_mixed_t22p0_rh35, most consistently stressed room Dining Room (96.1% mean PPD).
  • Young Scholars Male Only: highest mean PMV at young_male_t25p0_rh75, highest single-room PPD at young_male_t22p0_rh35, most consistently stressed room Dining Room (80.4% mean PPD).
  • Age-Shifted +50 Years: highest mean PMV at older_shift_t25p0_rh75, highest single-room PPD at older_shift_t22p0_rh35, most consistently stressed room Dining Room (100.0% mean PPD).

Young Scholars Mixed

Mixed male/female cohort, ages 25-45, one occupant per occupied room.
